untranslatable_reason: CPPI is a dynamic portfolio insurance algorithm requiring real-time portfolio value tracking, floor value calculations, leverage multiplier adjustments based on cushion ratios, and position sizing proportional to (Portfolio Value - Floor Value) / (D × Portfolio Value). These require stateful portfolio-level computations and dynamic leverage allocation that cannot be expressed as simple indicator-based entry/exit conditions. The signal requires: (1) tracking cumulative portfolio value relative to a dynamic floor, (2) calculating leverage = (Portfolio Value - Floor) / (Cushion), and (3) rebalancing position size continuously. Standard OHLCV indicators and technical overlays cannot replicate this algorithm without portfolio state management.
translation_notes: CPPI is a sophisticated portfolio insurance mechanism that operates at the portfolio allocation level rather than individual security signals. It requires: Current Portfolio Value tracking, Floor Value = Initial Value × (1 - D), Cushion = Portfolio Value - Floor, and Multiplier = Allocation / (D × Current Value). The stop_loss_pct of 50% corresponds to the D=0.5 example parameter. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because CPPI dynamically adjusts long exposure based on portfolio cushion. Backtest period noted as 100,000 days simulated.
